title: How Elon Musk Squeezed OpenAI: They 'Are Gonna Want to Kill Me’
description: Tensions flared on the third day of trial in Musk v. Altman as OpenAI’s lawyers cross-examined Musk.

title: Emergency First Responders Say Waymos Are Getting Worse
description: “I believe the technology was deployed too quickly in too vast amounts, with hundreds of vehicles, when it wasn’t really ready,” one police official told federal regulators last month.

title: Taylor Swift Wants to Trademark Her Likeness. These TikTok Deepfake Ads Show Why
description: Researchers show scammers are using AI-manipulated footage of celebrity interviews to trick users into sharing their personal data.

title: Sanctioned Chinese AI Firm SenseTime Releases Image Model Built for Speed
description: With US restrictions limiting its access to advanced tech, SenseTime is doubling down on open source with a new model optimized to run on Chinese-made chips.

title: When Robots Have Their ChatGPT Moment, Remember These Pincers
description: From sorting chicken nuggets to screwing in light bulbs, Eka’s robots are eerily lifelike. But do they have real physical smarts?

title: How AI Could Help Combat Antibiotic Resistance
description: At WIRED Health, British surgeon Ara Darzi said AI is set to transform the diagnosis and treatment of drug-resistant infections. But a lack of incentives means innovation may not reach patients.

title: OpenAI Really Wants Codex to Shut Up About Goblins | WIRED
description: “Never talk about goblins, gremlins, raccoons, trolls, ogres, pigeons, or other animals or creatures unless it is absolutely and unambiguously relevant,” reads OpenAI’s coding agent instructions.

title: Elon Musk Testifies That He Started OpenAI to Prevent a ‘Terminator Outcome’ | WIRED
description: The judge also warned Musk and Sam Altman to curb their “propensity to use social media to make things worse outside the courtroom” after both sides traded attacks online.

title: ‘It’s Undignified’: Hundreds of Workers Training Meta’s AI Could Be Laid Off | WIRED
description: More than 700 people working for a Meta contractor in Ireland are at risk of losing their jobs, documents show.

title: The Race Is on to Keep AI Agents From Running Wild With Your Credit Cards | WIRED
description: AI agents may soon be buying your stuff for you. The FIDO Alliance has teamed up with Google and Mastercard to try to ensure that shopping in the near future isn't a complete disaster.

title: The Bloomberg Terminal Is Getting an AI Makeover, Like It or Not | WIRED
description: WIRED spoke with Bloomberg's chief technology officer about the big, chatbot-style changes coming to the iconic platform for traders.

title: Some Musk v. Altman Jurors Don't Like Elon Musk | WIRED
description: Musk’s lawsuit challenges OpenAI’s evolution under Sam Altman. But during jury selection, several potential jurors voiced negative views of Musk himself.

title: Elon Musk Boosts New Yorker’s Sam Altman Exposé on X as Trial Begins | WIRED
description: The move comes as the trial for Elon Musk’s lawsuit against OpenAI kicks off in federal court in Oakland.

title: The Man Behind AlphaGo Thinks AI Is Taking the Wrong Path | WIRED
description: David Silver has a new billion-dollar company that aims to build AI “superlearners.”

title: Discord Sleuths Gained Unauthorized Access to Anthropic’s Mythos | WIRED
description: Plus: Spy firms tap into a global telecom weakness to track targets, 500,000 UK health records go up for sale on Alibaba, Apple patches a revealing notification bug, and more.

title: Ace the Ping-Pong Robot Can Whup Your Ass | WIRED
description: Ace can read the trajectory of a ball, adjust the racket angle, and respond with strokes that keep the exchange alive with real players.

title: AI-Designed Drugs by a DeepMind Spinoff Are Headed to Human Trials | WIRED
description: Isomorphic Labs president Max Jaderberg said at WIRED Health in London that the startup has built a “broad and exciting pipeline of new medicines.”

title: Apple's Next CEO Needs to Launch a Killer AI Product | WIRED
description: Tim Cook was a great CEO, but he didn’t crack AI.  It’s job number 1 for John Ternus.

title: These AI Thirst Trap Creators Say They’re Misunderstood | WIRED
description: A viral red carpet moment shone light on a group of hunky Instagram influencers—and the followers who are too horny to care that they’re not real.

title: 5 Reasons to Think Twice Before Using ChatGPT—or Any Chatbot—for Financial Advice | WIRED
description: As people increasingly rely on AI chatbots for guidance, even on financial matters, a healthy dose of skepticism is critical.

title: Apple’s Next Chapter, SpaceX and Cursor Strike a Deal, and Palantir’s Controversial Manifesto | WIRED
description: In this week’s episode of “Uncanny Valley,” we talk about Tim Cook’s legacy as CEO at Apple and what his long-rumored departure means for the future of one of the world's biggest companies.
